Care services offered in assisted living and memory care include medication management, assistance with grooming, dressing, bathing, continence care, ambulation, and more."}, {"question": "How is the level of care determined?", "answer": "As you prepare to move to Ivy Living, our team will provide you with a form called a Physician’s Report. Your primary care physician will use this form to provide us with any medical information that will help us determine how we can best support you. Along with the Physician’s Report, you will meet with our Health Service Director for a level of care assessment. During this meeting, you and the Health Service Director will discuss your needs and create a plan of care."}, {"question": "What if my care needs change?", "answer": "The goal at each Ivy Living community is for our residents to be able to continue to live with us as their needs change. web_faqs: [{"question": "What is hospice care?", "answer": "Hospice care is specialized care that focuses on providing comfort, support, and quality of life for patients with terminal illnesses and their families."}, {"question": "Who is eligible for hospice care?", "answer": "Patients with a terminal illness, typically with a prognosis of six months or less, and who are ready to focus on comfort rather than curative treatments, are eligible for hospice care."}, {"question": "Is hospice care only provided in hospitals?", "answer": "No. Hospice care can be provided at home, in nursing facilities, assisted living centers, and hospitals. In-home hospice is the most common setting."}, {"question": "What services does hospice care include?", "answer": "Hospice care includes pain and symptom management, emotional and spiritual support, caregiver training, respite care, and bereavement support for families."}, {"question": "Does hospice care cover medications and equipment?", "answer": "Yes. Most hospice programs provide medications related to the terminal illness, medical equipment, and supplies needed to ensure comfort and quality of life."}, {"question": "How long can someone receive hospice care?", "answer": "Hospice care can be provided for as long as the patient meets eligibility requirements. Some patients receive care for weeks, while others may receive it for several months."}, {"question": "Can patients continue to see their regular doctors while on hospice?", "answer": "Yes. Patients often continue to see their regular doctors for ongoing care, while the hospice team coordinates to ensure comfort and symptom management."}, {"question": "Is hospice care covered by insurance or Medicare?", "answer": "Most hospice care services are covered by Medicare, Medicaid, and many private insurance plans. Coverage usually includes medications, equipment, and nursing visits related to the terminal illness."}, {"question": "What is the difference between hospice care and palliative care?", "answer": "Palliative care focuses on relief from symptoms and stress at any stage of serious illness, while hospice care is specifically for patients who are nearing the end of life and are no longer seeking curative treatments."}, {"question": "Can hospice care be provided at home?", "answer": "Yes. Home hospice care is the most common setting, allowing patients to receive comfort-focused care in a familiar environment, supported by nurses, aides, and social workers."}, {"question": "What areas does Good Heart Hospice serve?", "answer": "We provide hospice and palliative care services to families in San Bernardino and Riverside."}, {"question": "Does Good Heart Hospice serve other nearby counties as well?", "answer": "Yes, we can serve nearby counties including Los Angeles through our trusted partner Affinity Hospice LLC. Learn more at https://affinityhospicellc.com."}, {"question": "Hospice Care", "answer": "Comprehensive care for patients with life-limiting illnesses, focusing on comfort, dignity, and quality of life. Learn more about hospice."}, {"question": "Palliative Care", "answer": "Specialized care for individuals managing serious illnesses, aimed at relieving symptoms and improving quality of life at any stage of illness. web_facility_name: Historic Cucamonga Service Station | Route 66 Inland Empire California | Museum
web_about: The Cucamonga Service Station is an award winning, nationally recognized Route 66 landmark. Built in 1915 it has been restored to its original look as a Richfield station of the 1930s and is now open to the public as a museum with artifacts of local and Route 66 history. For more detailed information, download the official guide (PDF).
